---
title: "Author plates"
format: html
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Author plates}
  %\VignetteEngine{quarto::html}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

## Overview

The **author-plate** filter generates formatted author blocks for academic documents. It creates a professional author list with affiliations, superscript numbers, and optional corresponding author markers.

## Basic usage

Add an author-plate div to your document where you want the author block to appear:

```markdown
---
title: "My Research Paper"
docstyle:
  authors:
    - name:
        given: "Jane"
        family: "Smith"
      affiliations:
        - ref: univ
      corresponding: true
      email: "jane@university.edu"
    - name:
        given: "John"
        family: "Doe"
      affiliations:
        - ref: hospital
  affiliations:
    - id: univ
      name: "University of Example"
      city: "Boston"
      region: "MA"
      country: "USA"
    - id: hospital
      name: "General Hospital"
      city: "Boston"
      region: "MA"
      country: "USA"
---

::: author-plate
:::

## Introduction

Your content here...
```

This produces:

> **Jane Smith**^1,\*^, **John Doe**^2^
>
> ^1^ University of Example, Boston, MA, USA
> ^2^ General Hospital, Boston, MA, USA
>
> \*Corresponding author: jane@university.edu

### Placing the abstract below the plate

For an academic preprint, the abstract usually sits just below the author block. Add an `abstract:` front-matter key and a `::: docstyle-abstract :::` placeholder right after the plate:

```markdown
::: author-plate
:::

::: docstyle-abstract
:::
```

In `docstyle-docx` this relocates the abstract from the top of the document (where Pandoc places it by default) to the placeholder position. It is docx-only — Typst and JATS render `abstract:` natively above/within their title areas — and opt-in: without the placeholder the abstract stays at the top. See the [YAML schema reference](reference-yaml-schema.html#abstract) for details. (Not to be confused with the `.abstract` CSS class, which only *styles* abstract text.)

## Presets

Different journals and conferences have different author block formats. Use presets to quickly match common styles:

```yaml
docstyle:
  author-plate:
    preset: smdm-abstract
```

### Available presets

| Preset | Alignment | Email shown | ORCID shown | Use case |
|--------|-----------|-------------|-------------|----------|
| `manuscript` | center | yes | no | Standard journal manuscripts |
| `smdm-abstract` | left | no | no | SMDM conference abstracts |
| `plos` | left | yes | yes | PLOS journals |

### Preset details

**`manuscript`** (default behaviour)
- Center-aligned author and affiliation lines
- Shows corresponding author email
- Standard academic manuscript format

**`smdm-abstract`**
- Left-aligned for conference abstract formatting
- Hides email (not needed in abstracts)
- Compact presentation

**`plos`**
- Left-aligned per PLOS style guide
- Shows both email and ORCID identifiers
- Full attribution format

## Configuration options

You can customize the author plate beyond presets:

```yaml
docstyle:
  author-plate:
    preset: smdm-abstract       # Start from a preset
    align: left                 # Override: left, center, right
    show-email: false           # Override: hide email
    show-orcid: true            # Override: show ORCID
    corresponding-marker: "*"   # Symbol for corresponding author
    equal-marker: "†"           # Symbol for equal contributors
```

Explicit options override preset defaults.

## Author metadata

### Required fields

```yaml
authors:
  - name:
      given: "First"
      family: "Last"
    affiliations:
      - ref: inst1    # Reference to affiliation id
```

### Optional fields

```yaml
authors:
  - name:
      given: "First"
      family: "Last"
    affiliations:
      - ref: inst1
      - ref: inst2    # Multiple affiliations
    corresponding: true
    email: "author@example.com"
    orcid: "0000-0000-0000-0000"
    equal-contributor: true
```

### Affiliation format

```yaml
affiliations:
  - id: inst1                 # Unique identifier for ref:
    name: "Institution Name"  # Required
    department: "Department"  # Optional
    city: "City"              # Optional
    region: "State/Province"  # Optional
    country: "Country"        # Optional
```

The display string is built as: `Department, Name, City, Region, Country` (omitting missing parts).

## Important: Configuration location

**Author-plate configuration must be in the QMD front matter**, not in `_quarto.yml`.

This is because Quarto processes extension configuration before passing it to Lua filters, and nested `docstyle.*` keys in `_quarto.yml` are not passed through.

**Correct (in .qmd file):**

```yaml
---
title: "My Document"
docstyle:
  author-plate:
    preset: smdm-abstract
  authors:
    - name: ...
  affiliations:
    - id: ...
---
```

**Incorrect (in _quarto.yml):**

```yaml
# This won't work - config is stripped before reaching Lua filter
docstyle:
  author-plate:
    preset: smdm-abstract
```

## CSS styling

The author plate uses two Word styles that you can customize in CSS:

```css
/* Author names line */
.author {
  font-family: "Times New Roman", Times, serif;
  font-size: 12pt;
  font-weight: bold;
}

/* Affiliation lines */
.affiliation {
  font-family: "Times New Roman", Times, serif;
  font-size: 12pt;
  font-weight: normal;
  font-style: normal;  /* or italic for some journals */
}
```

These styles control typography. The preset controls layout (alignment, what to show).

## Debug mode

To see what the filter is doing:

```bash
DOCSTYLE_DEBUG=1 quarto render document.qmd
```

Output includes:
```
[author-plate] Found 4 authors (from by-author)
[author-plate] Found 3 affiliations (from by-affiliation)
[author-plate] Loading preset: smdm-abstract
[author-plate] Generating author plate with 4 authors
```

## Examples

### Conference abstract (SMDM style)

```yaml
---
title: "Study Results"
author:
  - name:
      given: "Jane"
      family: "Smith"
    affiliations:
      - ref: ohri
    corresponding: true
    email: "jane@ohri.ca"
affiliations:
  - id: ohri
    name: "Ottawa Hospital Research Institute"
    city: "Ottawa"
    region: "ON"
    country: "Canada"
docstyle:
  author-plate:
    preset: smdm-abstract
---
```

### Journal manuscript (centered, with email)

```yaml
---
title: "Research Findings"
author:
  - name:
      given: "John"
      family: "Doe"
    affiliations:
      - ref: univ
      - ref: hospital
    corresponding: true
    email: "john.doe@university.edu"
  - name:
      given: "Jane"
      family: "Smith"
    affiliations:
      - ref: univ
    equal-contributor: true
affiliations:
  - id: univ
    name: "University Medical School"
    department: "Department of Medicine"
    city: "Boston"
    region: "MA"
    country: "USA"
  - id: hospital
    name: "Teaching Hospital"
    city: "Boston"
    region: "MA"
    country: "USA"
docstyle:
  author-plate:
    align: center
    show-email: true
---
```

## Creating custom presets

If you frequently need a format not covered by built-in presets, you can:

1. Use explicit options to override a similar preset
2. Request a new preset be added via GitHub issue

Custom presets can be added to the Lua filter at `_extensions/docstyle/author-plate.lua` in the `presets` table.

## Multi-format projects (docx + PDF)

When a project renders to multiple formats (e.g. `docstyle-docx` and `preprint-typst`), define authors and affiliations **once** at the top level of `_quarto.yml`. Quarto normalizes these into `by-author` and `by-affiliation`, which `author-plate.lua` reads for the Word output and which `preprint-typst` uses for the PDF.

```yaml
# _quarto.yml — single definition, works for all output formats
format:
  docstyle-docx:
    toc: false
  preprint-typst:
    running-head: "My Paper"
    bibliography: references.bib
    csl: https://www.zotero.org/styles/vancouver

authors:
  - name:
      given: Jane
      family: Smith
    orcid: 0000-0000-0000-0001
    affiliations:
      - ref: inst1
    corresponding: true
    email: jsmith@example.com
    roles:                       # CRediT taxonomy — passed through for future use
      - conceptualization
      - writing – original draft

affiliations:
  - id: inst1
    name: University of Ottawa
    department: School of Epidemiology and Public Health
    city: Ottawa
    region: Ontario
    country: Canada

docstyle:
  author-plate:
    corresponding-marker: '*'
    show-email: true
  # other docstyle options...
```

The `roles:` field (CRediT taxonomy) is stored as a passthrough custom field in `by-author`. It is not rendered in the current release but will be used for CRediT statement generation in a future version.

### Migrating from `docstyle.authors`

If your project currently uses `docstyle.authors:` and `docstyle.affiliations:`, remove those blocks and move the content to top-level `authors:` and `affiliations:`. Add any `roles:` entries directly to each author. The deprecation warning `[author-plate] Warning: docstyle.authors is deprecated` indicates the old pattern is still in use.
